# AI and Collective Intelligence (MASC-AICI-01)

## Module Overview

This module explores the intersection of artificial intelligence and collective human behaviour. Students will examine how AI systems can augment, replicate, or undermine group decision-making processes.

## Learning Outcomes

1. Critically analyse theories of collective intelligence and their relationship to AI
2. Evaluate the design of AI systems that support group decision-making
3. Demonstrate understanding of cognitive biases and their implications for AI design
4. Design and prototype a collective intelligence tool

## Teaching Schedule

| Week | Topic | Format |
|------|-------|--------|
| 1 | Introduction to Collective Intelligence | Lecture |
| 2 | Wisdom of Crowds | Lecture + Seminar |
| 3 | Heuristics and Biases | Lecture |
| 4 | Social Influence and Information Cascades | Lecture + Workshop |
| 5 | Swarm Intelligence (Guest: Dr. Maria Lopez) | Guest Lecture |
| 6 | AI-Augmented Decision Making | Workshop |
| 7 | Designing Collective Intelligence Systems | Workshop |
| 8 | Ethics of AI in Group Settings | Seminar |
| 9 | Project Presentations | Assessment |
| 10 | Review and Reflection | Seminar |

## Assessment

- Individual Essay (40%) — Due Week 7
- Group Project (60%) — Due Week 9
